book_summary: Leviticus has chapters 1-16 complete in restored/source-audited draft and mystical companion layers. The opening offering manual is framed as ancient ritual grammar for drawing near after the tabernacle is filled with glory. Chapters 8-10 narrate priestly ordination, glory, rupture, grief, and discernment. Chapters 11-15 cover clean/unclean animals, childbirth purification, scale-disease/tzaraat diagnosis and cleansing, garment/house affliction, and bodily discharges with strong anti-shame and body-honoring safeguards. Chapter 16 gives the Day of Atonement as sanctuary cleansing, communal burden-removal, priestly entry boundary, Sabbath-rest, and yearly restoration before YHWH.

translation_notes: Leviticus continues Exodus: YHWH calls from the completed tent of meeting.
Korban/offering means something brought near; offerings are approach/nearness grammar, not crude appeasement.
Blood/life should be treated as ritual life-symbolism, not gore spectacle.
Clean/unclean language is ritual-state language, not body shame, moral disgust, disability stigma, or human worth ranking.
Spiritual repair does not replace material restitution. Leviticus 6 requires return plus added fifth before/with offering.
Priestly ordination is accountable service, not status entitlement.
Moses accepts Aaron's grief-shaped refusal to eat the offering; grief can interrupt ritual expectation.
Leviticus 11 dietary distinctions are ritual food/handling instruction within Israel's covenant identity, not disgust toward animals or peoples.
Leviticus 12 childbirth impurity is ritual-state language tied to blood/life and sanctuary approach, not moral fault, sexual shame, or disgust toward mother or baby.
Tzaraat is not modern leprosy/Hansen's disease by default; render as scale-disease/skin affliction pending lexical audit.
Priest functions diagnostically/ritually in Leviticus 13-14, not as moral accuser.
Quarantine/outside-camp language must not become illness stigma or dehumanization.
Leviticus 15 treats semen, menstruation, and genital discharges as ritual contact states affecting sanctuary approach, not as sin in themselves.
Bodies, flows, birth, illness, sex, and menstruation are not evil; washing, waiting, and offerings protect sanctuary boundaries without degrading bodies.
Leviticus 16 is explicitly framed after Nadab and Abihu; entry boundaries are about dangerous nearness to holy presence.
Kippur/atonement includes cleansing sanctuary from accumulated uncleannesses and transgressions, not only individual guilt relief.
Azazel is preserved without over-definition; removal goat carries iniquities away into wilderness/cut-off land.
Self-affliction/humbling and no work apply to native and sojourner alike; communal cleansing includes resident outsider.
Mystical companion layer remains interpretive and does not replace source-audited restored text.
source_witness_audit_flags: Leviticus/Vayiqra title and opening call from tent requires Torah continuity crosslink with Exodus 40.
Korban/nearness terminology requires lexical audit across offering texts.
Chatat as sin/purification offering requires deep lexical and ritual-function audit.
Clean/unclean terminology requires anti-shame and ritual-state metadata across Leviticus.
Leviticus 10 Nadab and Abihu requires ritual-boundary, grief, and anti-spiritual-abuse reception-history audit.
Leviticus 11 animal taxonomy and prohibited terms require lexical, zoological, and ritual audit.
Leviticus 12 childbirth purification durations require source, gender-ethics, and anti-shame audit.
Leviticus 13-14 tzaraat/scale-disease terminology requires lexical, medical-history, and anti-stigma audit.
Leviticus 15 bodily discharge terminology requires anti-sex-shame, anti-menstrual-shame, and ritual-state audit.
Leviticus 16 kippur/atonement terminology requires sanctuary-cleansing and covering lexical audit.
Leviticus 16 Azazel requires deep source, lexical, and reception-history audit without speculative overclaiming.
Leviticus 16 self-affliction/humbling requires fasting/ritual practice audit and anti-self-hatred safeguard.
next_actions: Continue Leviticus with chapters 17-20: blood/life, slaughter centralization, sexual-boundary material, holiness/social ethics, child-sacrifice polemic, and severe penalty texts with strong anti-weaponization safeguards.
